chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, BENCH AT SUKKUR C.P.No. D- 1645 of 2021 Date of hearing Order with signature of Judge. Hearing of Case 1.For orders on o/objection 2.For hearing of main case 01-03-2022 Mr. Amjad Ali Keerio, Advocate for the Petitioner. Mr. Mehboob Ali Wassan, Assistant A.G. ***** Through this Petition, the Petitioner seeks directions for payment of pension and other benefits to the Petitioner. It is case of the Petitioner that the Petitioner’s husband was appointed as Constable in Sindh Police and expired on 27.07.2009. Notices were ordered and comments have been filed, wherein reply to paras-2&3 is as under: “2. As per record of Sheet Branch of this Office that there the Original Service of late PC/11480 Ali Sher is not available. The photocopy of service book is also silent about Mst. Dadli (Petitioner) to be the wife of above named deceased constable. 3. Record shows that PC/11480 Ali Sher was appointed as Constable in Sindh Reserve Police on 12.10.1986. He was however removed from service by SP/SRP Sukkur vide order dated SSC/SRP/3937-89 dated 24-06-1996, on the allegations of absent from duty w.e.from 08-12-1995 to 03-02-1996 and again remained absent from duty from 04-02-1996 onwards. It was also alleged that he (deceased) took away Government Rifle with 100 rounds. This office record is also silent whether PC/1480 Ali Sher had filed an appeal to the DIGP/SRP Sindh Karachi in the year 2003 and re-instated in service in 2009. The petitioner has not quoted the order of reinstatement in service and date passed by the authority in this behalf. There is also no record in office to show whether PC/11480 Ali Sher had drawn salaries uptil June/July, 2009”.

The petitioner has not quoted the order of reinstatement in service and date passed by the authority in this behalf. There is also no record in office to show whether PC/11480 Ali Sher had drawn salaries uptil June/July, 2009”. Perusal of aforesaid comments reflects that insofar as Petitioner’s husband is concerned, as per record of the Respondents, he was removed from service on the ground of absence without leave; whereas, there are further allegations against the Petitioner’s husband as well. It has further been stated that there is no record of any reinstatement of the Petitioner’s husband, as contended; whereas, the Petitioner has also not supported such pleadings by any order of reinstatement. In view of such position, the Petitioner does not appear to be entitled for any relief from this Court. Even otherwise, the Petitioner’s husband expired in the year 2009 and Petitioner approached this Court in the year 2021. In view of above, no case is made out. Accordingly, this Petition stands dismissed. JUDGE JUDGE Ahmad
